SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS IN OPPOS TO MTN FOR SUMM JUDGMNT FILED BY DEFENDANT KLEEGE INDUSTRIES, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS KLEEGE INDUSTRIES)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AND DENIED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS PRODUCED EVIDENCE THAT IT WAS A SPECIAL EMPLOYER OF PLAINTIFF PEREZ. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF CROSS-DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 10-18; COMPARE WITH RILEY V. SOUTHWEST MARINE, INC. (1988) 203 CAL.APP.3D 1242, 1250.) AS A CONSEQUENCE OF ESTABLISHING A SPECIAL EMPLOYER RELATIONSHIP, THE SPECIAL EMPLOYER IS IMMUNE FROM A COMMON LAW TORT ACTION. (SANTA CRUZ POULTRY, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 194 CAL.APP.3D 575, 578.) AS SUCH, THE BURDEN SHIFT TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT KLEEGE INDUSTRIES, INC. TO DEMONSTRATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT EXISTS. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN AS IT HAS NOT PRODUCED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO CONTROVERT. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHIFTED THE BURDEN. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS NOT PRODUCED PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS BEEN (OR WILL BE) PAID IN FULL FOR ANY LOSS. (SEE UMF 22-24; COMPARE WITH PATENT SCAFFOLDING CO. V. WILLIAM SIMPSON CONST. (1967) 256 CAL.APP.2D 506, 508.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
ORDER RE [CROSS-DEFENDANT ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC'S] MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No appearance. The Court takes DEFENDANT KLEEGE INDUSTRIES, INC.'s Motion For Leave To Amend Response To Request For Admissions off calendar at the request of the moving party. Moving party informed the court via email that the case has settled. Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: STEPHEN DAHL, ESQ., 650.991.5126 X12, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT KLEEGE INDUSTRIES.
ROBERT BRAGG, ESQ., 415.273.6500, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR summary adjudication is granted as to the first, second, and third causes of action and denied as to the fourth cause of action. As to the first, second, and third causes of action, Cross-Defendant has produced evidence that it was a special employer of Plaintiff Perez. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Cross-Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 10-18; compare with Riley v. Southwest Marine, Inc. (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1242, 1250.) As a consequence of establishing a special employer relationship, the special employer is immune from a common law tort action. (Santa Cruz Poultry, Inc. v. Superior Court (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 575, 578.) As such, the burden shift to Cross-Complainant Kleege Industries, Inc. to demonstrate a triable issue of material fact exists. Cross-Defendant has not carried its burden as it has not produced sufficient evidence to controvert. As to the fourth cause of action, Cross-Defendant has not shifted the burden. Cross-Defendant has not produced prima facie evidence that Cross-Complainant has been (or will be) paid in full for any loss. (See UMF 22-24; compare with Patent Scaffolding Co. v. William Simpson Const. (1967) 256 Cal.App.2d 506, 508.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
